note:
在此之前，Aarmy一直在纽约和洛杉矶的位置提供面对面的健身课程。 Trey Laird与培训师Akin Akman（首席健身官）和Angela Manuel-Davis（首席激励官）一起创办了这家初创公司，他告诉我，这种策略在48小时内开始转变为在线模式，从通过Instagram Live进行健身课程开始，这种情况一直持续下去。提供，同时在夏季推出数字订阅程序。
 这家初创公司得到了包括Jay-Z，Chris Paul和Karlie Kloss等名人投资者以及Mousse Partners，Valia Ventures，Pendulum和Wilshire Lane Partners等公司的支持。 Laird说，该团队一直计划开展在线业务，其中一些物理位置充当“内容引擎”。大流行只是加速了那些计划。
  “改变的是，我们认为我们有时间完善所有东西，”阿克曼说。 “ [一旦大流行爆发，]我们没有时间进行所有这些深入的对话，我们没有时间等待。我们想去那里。”
 Aarmy订阅的费用为每月$ 35或每年$ 350，可访问完整的数字图书馆，包括与Aarmy培训师进行的实时课程，每周上传20个新的练习课程。该公司表示，已经有成千上万的付费用户，免费试用的转换率超过70％，总体保留率达到88％。
  戴维斯（Davis）承认，阿米（Aarmy）的教练已经重新考虑了他们的做法，特别是因为他们无法按原计划“在60人的房间里”射击自己。现在，他们必须自己提供所有的能量，并且他们需要“超级有意”地计划会议，而不是简单地响应房间里的运动员的活动。
 除了这些课程外，Aarmy还开展了服装业务，在其自己的网站上以及通过Net a Porter销售各种健身器材。实际上，该公司表示，这方面的业务已经带来了450,000美元的销售额。
 艾金（Akin）将整个Aarmy策略描述为一种既涉及心理调节又涉及身体适应的策略-他认为这种策略非常适合大流行时代，当时许多人都在为沮丧，孤独和他们是“情况的受害者”。 
Laird补充说：“对于一个品牌，其灵感和精神力量以及发现灵感与实际运动或实际锻炼同样重要的品牌来说，这是完美的时机。 这为与世界各地的人们建立联系并展示与众不同之处提供了绝佳的机会。”
note_en:
Up until then, Aarmy was offering in-person fitness classes from its locations in New York and Los Angeles. Trey Laird, who founded the startup with trainers Akin Akman (chief fitness officer) and Angela Manuel-Davis (chief motivation officer), told me that within 48 hours, the strategy shifted online, starting with fitness classes via  Instagram Live — something it continues to offer, while also  launching a digital subscription program over the summer.
 The startup is backed by celebrity investors including Jay-Z, Chris Paul and Karlie Kloss, as well as firms like Mousse Partners, Valia Ventures, Pendulum and Wilshire Lane Partners. Laird said that the team always planned to launch an online business, with a few physical locations serving as “content engines.” The pandemic just accelerated those plans.
  “What changed is, we thought we had time to perfect everything,” Akman said. “[Once the pandemic hit,] we didn’t have time to have all these in-depth conversations, we didn’t have time to wait. We wanted to get out there.”
 An Aarmy subscription costs $35 a month or $350 a year, offering access to a full digital library, including live sessions with Aarmy trainers, with 20 new practice sessions uploaded every week. The company says it already has “thousands” of paying subscribers, with a conversion rate of more than 70% from its free trial, and an 88% retention rate overall.
  Davis acknowledged that Aarmy’s coaches have had rethink their approach, particularly since they can’t shoot themselves “in a room with 60 people” as originally planned. Now they have to provide all of the energy themselves, and they need to be “super intentional” about planning their sessions, rather than simply responding to the activity of the athletes in the room.
 Beyond the classes, Aarmy has also launched an apparel business, selling a variety of fitness gear  on its own website and via Net a Porter. In fact, the company says this side of the business has already brought in $450,000 in sales.
 Akin described the overall Aarmy strategy as one that’s as much about mental conditioning as it is about physical fitness — which he argued has been well-suited to the pandemic era, when so many people are struggling with feelings of depression, isolation and the sense that they’re “victims of circumstance.”
 Laird added, “For a brand where the inspiration and the mental strength and finding that inspiration is as important as the actual movement or actual workout, it’s been the perfect time. It’s presented a great opportunity to connect with people around the world and show what differentiates us.”
title: 健身初创公司Aarmy重新打造了一个远程健身世界
title_en: Fitness startup Aarmy reinvents itself for a remote fitness world

晕！我最近从Purism获得了我的Librem 5（常绿）。 Librem 5是运行其他标准Linux内核的智能手机。然而，与Android也依赖于引擎盖下的Linux内核不同，Librem 5使用了GNU用户空间并适合移动设备，这使它在某种程度上更类似于典型的笔记本电脑，尽管其外形仍然类似于现代智能手机（至少，大部分）。以下是有关这款手机以及与Pine64的Pinephone（与另一款既不使用Android也不使用iOS并依赖于GNU / Linux操作系统的手机）进行比较的初步想法。
 这是一篇很长的文章，对此我感到抱歉。精简无疑是一项来之不易的技能，但是如果不将Librem 5与Panephone相提并论，继续前进似乎是错误的选择；
   Purism宣称Librem 5为“注重安全性和隐私性的手机”。这并非完全不正确，在这方面，Librem 5具有一些独特的功能。例如，电话的一侧有“ kill-switchs”，可让您禁用：
  还有一项附加功能，如果您禁用所有三个开关，那么手机上的其他传感器（例如GPS）也将全部禁用。您最好阅读Librem 5产品页面，以了解更多信息。
  与吹捧自己注重隐私和安全性的Librem 5相比，Pinephone被吹捧为“所有主要Linux Phone项目支持的AnOpen Source智能电话”。从硬件一直到最终软件，它在很多方面都与Librem 5有所不同。它也有杀伤开关，但是这些不容易在手机侧面触及，而是在手机外壳内。您可以通过卸下后面板来访问这些终止开关。
 Pinephone的一件整洁的事情之一是能够在硬件本身上引导和使用各种Linux发行版。就像将发行版加载到SD卡上，然后启动手机一样简单。您甚至不必刷新任何内容即可运行内部eMMC！
   Librem 5的起价为$ 799.00 USD，如果您想在A的优质美国组装Librem 5，然后再将其发货给您，则可以$ 1999.00的价格购买。关于反拦截或控制供应链的话题很多，但是请相信我，您肯定不会在市场上买到像Librem 5这样的价位为2K的手机。我不知道是谁。 
我可能会写一篇关于这方面经济学的完整文章，但坦白说，这真是让人感到震惊，任何人都会为此付出如此之高。令人震惊的是，纯粹主义会收取这么多费用。我可以想像一个方案，其中零件从中国运出，并由美国的非全日制大学生验证和组装，他们可能仍能以比1999.00美元便宜的价格定价。
 无论如何，这篇文章不是关于Purism的商业惯例的，我会在某个时候发表评论，但是无论如何我都会在此发表。请不要在Librem 5上花费$ 1999.00。
 对于那个最终会说他们确实购买了$ 1999.00美国版的人，这对于他们的用例来说是非常必要的：伙计，我明白了，您在这上喝了Kool-Aid，但我对此表示怀疑即使对于那些对FOSS和移动硬件的交叉感到兴奋的普通书呆子，您的非常具体的关注和用例也适用。而且，我怀疑，即使它们确实适用于这样的小众群体，也就是$ 2k是一个价位，您仍然不愿以这个价位去寻找其他字面意义上的东西。
 我在最初的众筹活动中买回了Librem 5，因此最终花费比799美元还少得多。出乎意料的是，自2017年底以来，我一直在等待这款手机，而我们现在已经在2020年了，如果有的话。 Purism一直在发出有效的DVT和EVT电话运行，这些运行已经经历了几个阶段，分别是Aspen，Birch，Chestnut，Dogwood，最后是Evergreen。 Evergreen应该是我选择的硬件的最终版本。我想获得最终的，真正的Librem 5，而不是一个完全不同的产品。虽然我的设备没有FCC认证（从技术上讲，您可以将其视为PVT单元），但它代表了Purism将在此手机上使用的最终硬件，因为表面上没有更多的硬件版本。
  Pinephone的起价为149.99美元，围绕特定的发行版/社区小批量出售，该发行版/社区正在为该设备构建软件（称为“发行版名称”社区版）。最近的批次已提供“融合版本”，价格为199.99美元。融合版本具有千兆字节的RAM和更大的eMMC闪存，被吹捧为将Pinephone连接到全尺寸显示器并像运行完整台式机一样运行它很有用。
 我最初是从UBPorts CE批次购买的Pinephone。最终，我升级了主板，以使我的规格与以后批次的融合版本的规格相匹配。总而言之，我的支出已经超过了原来的200美元，但我并不失望，因为我在thePinephone上玩得很开心。
  对于绝大多数人来说，Librem 5和Pinephone甚至都不值得考虑。 GNU用户空间和其中的关联“移动”范式几乎还没有准备好用于日常使用。 
对于Librem 5，此“针对安全性和隐私性的电话”在某些安全方面甚至没有发挥作用（例如，硬件支持的安全性/安全保护区，设备加密等）。并不是说这些东西在将来的更新中不可能实现，但是就今天的软件故事而言，它们还不存在。
  无论如何，作者为什么要关心我？我承认，在我第一次使用后，这两个电话都不是我的日常司机。我可能会继续坚持使用我的Pixel 3，并且很有可能在未来两年内的某个时候最终获得另一台Android设备。 Linux手机肯定还不能成为日常驱动程序。但是，对于一些关键原因，它们很有趣。
  数字浪费是我们社会的一大祸害。我可以承认，逐年的硬件周期提供了大量改进，可以改变智能手机的面貌。但是，我也承认，越来越多的手机，平板电脑和其他电子设备会进入垃圾填埋场，否则会变成垃圾。 Linux手机和移动硬件为摆脱这种情况提供了机会，因此以某种方式支持这一点很重要。
  我是自由软件的迷！虽然我的日常工作不是使用FOSS，但我认为拥有更好的FOSS软件会带来很多好处。至少是因为移动领域的飞跃也吸引了其他人在台式机领域在Linux上开展工作。
  我很关心Linux手机，因为它们代表了Android / iOS双寡头的第三个选择。即使他们现在以很多方式吸吮，他们也会改善加班时间。我想迈向一个未来，无需在为数据收集和广告构建的OS与将您锁定在生态系统中的OS之间进行选择。
  首先，在比较两者的印象之前，我应该至少提一下我正在/未测试的内容。我没有/不会测试：
 电话和电话短信/彩信功能我没有辅助手机号码，我的主要手机号码已注册到Pixel 3上的电子SIM卡中。因此，我能做的最好的事情就是测试移动数据在纯数据SIM卡上的工作方式。 
融合功能除了移动设备外，我并没有打算将它们用作任何其他用途，因此，我没有费心将它们插入显示器。此外，我的显示器可能太大和高分辨率太大，以至于两部手机都无法有效驱动它。
  电子邮件此邮件仍在我的TODO列表中，但是我懒得尝试在Pinephone的一些发行版中进行设置。
  相机Librem 5尚无法正常使用相机。要让手机附带硬件但不附带软件……这不是典型的情况。因此，鉴于Librem 5尚未实现此功能，我无法进行任何比较。
  GPS /位置看，我想比任何人都做得更多，但是有了COVID，我几乎所有需要离开房屋的地方都需要导航。因此，我只比较这方面的表面特征。
  总的来说，我觉得Phosh使用起来很尴尬。我不喜欢感觉很平稳。这在启动应用程序抽屉或通知托盘的方式中最容易看出。您只需轻按即可，而无需刷卡（感觉非常自然）。感觉好像是通过这种方式完成的，因为类似按钮的行为比处理加速和滑动更容易。这可能很大程度上是个人喜好，但是我真的非常不喜欢Phosh作为基地。 Librem 5的UI替代品不如Pinephone多，部分原因在于：
  因此，对我来说很不幸，我暂时必须坚持使用Phosh的Librem 5。这在很大程度上是一个首选项问题，但这是一个问题。
 Lomiri是UBPorts / Ubuntutouch（最好是Manjaro ！！）发行版的Pinephone的UI的名称。这种UI感觉非常自然得多，我非常感谢一切基于滑动的行为。即使与现代Android及其所有怪异功能相比，我认为Lomiri对于如何与设备交互也具有正确的想法。 
尽管我更喜欢Lomiri而不是Phosh作为电话接口，但我的确要说这两个设备之间的处理能力差异非常明显。这是可以预料的，因为Pinephone便宜得多，但是Librem 5在如何处理触摸输入，滚动速度，延迟等方面表现出色。Lomiri做得很好，并且在Pinephone上不会感到慢；但是，Librem 5只是感觉一直更快。硬件和所有方面都更好，但是软件的故事似乎并不是从一个糟糕的地方开始的（当然，这与您在Evergreen之前阅读的内容可能有很大的不同，但是正在这里取得进步）。
 可以肯定地希望有一天，Librem 5将会有一个Lomiri前端。但是，我并没有屏息，因为似乎并没有很多发行广告宣传他们希望支持Librem5（再次）。 ，可能主要是由于价格）。我坚持住Phosh有点可耻，但嘿，Purism继续写着自己，那为什么不呢？我确实希望能提供类似Ubuntu Touch的东西，如果是这样的话，我可以看到软件故事大不相同。
  Librem 5上的屏幕键盘可以正常运行，并且可以完成工作，但可以完成一些工作。大多数Pinephone发行版也使用相同的键盘，因此这里没有大赢家。但是，Ubuntu Touch的键盘绝对是最重要的。它具有拼写更正，单词建议等事实，使它变得更好。
 同样，这是非常优先的事情，我希望键盘将来能收到更多更新。我看不出为什么不能这样做，或者为什么无法使用更高级的功能的根本原因。
 可能还值得注意的是，如果您对这些设备一无所知：刷卡输入不是Linux手机上任何键盘的功能。也许有一天，但现在不是。
  使Pinephone感到沮丧的一件事是，kill开关似乎是电话的次要功能。那是什么意思？基本上：这些终止开关实际上并没有很好地集成到软件中。实际上，我并不在乎您必须卸下后面板才能与其接触，但是有时我注意到该软件无法很好地处理可能会关闭其中一个的事实。到目前为止，我已经讲过很多有关Ubuntu Touch的知识，但是如果您禁用移动数据/基带开关，它确实不一样。其他发行版以各种方式处理此问题，但是特别是在我的UBPorts CE Pinephone拥有原始主板的情况下，禁用基带有时会使wifi进入深度睡眠状态（即，屏幕关闭了几个小时）后，wifi永远无法连接。 。
 我不是要调试此问题还是确定原因，但似乎这部手机上的取消开关已不是第1天的功能。鉴于我现在每天在家工作（这到底是一部手机吗？），这不是世界末日。但是，在这方面，Pinephone和Librem 5一样出色，我将不胜感激。 
值得注意的是，Librem 5 Kill开关似乎工作异常出色。之所以说“出现”，是因为基本上不可能测试相机和麦克风killswitch（主要是因为缺少功能正常的相机）。我还没有测试过任何通信应用程序，因为我不相信我现在正在使用任何通信应用程序。当两部手机上都有一个功能齐全的Matrix客户端时，我将很乐意进行测试：-)
 尽管如此，基带和wifi抑制开关完全可以实现Librem 5上的预期功能。此外，当关闭并再次打开它们时，设备和软件将恢复出色的性能。对于纯粹主义者而言，这是一个巨大的胜利，因为这绝非易事。
  这里没有太多要说的。 Google Fi的纯数据SIM卡在任何一部手机上都能正常运行。使用Pinephone或Librem 5连接网络时，我没有遇到任何重大问题。
  Linux手机上尚无广阔的应用程序生态系统。 Ubuntu Touch拥有最大的应用程序商店，但是坦率地说，其中许多应用程序并不是真正意义上的操盘手。但是，从我的第一印象来看，应用程序在某些方面还是很突出的。
  我知道我说过我不会讨论GPS的东西，因为我几乎没用过，但是我的基本第一印象是：
 Pinephone（带有uNav3）是一种令人愉悦的体验，但获得firstlocation锁可能要花很长时间。我相信这对于Pinephone硬件是唯一的，但是我不确定我是否做错了什么。
  Librem 5似乎默认为获取移动网络位置。它确实可以快速锁定位置，但似乎未使用GPS，因此显示不准确。这在尝试使用Lyft网络应用程序并使用“当前位置”设置我的提货位置时最为明显。我很高兴浏览器似乎支持此功能，但需要进行一些改进。 
Librem 5对网络应用程序的支持使我印象深刻。虽然我最初在浏览器上遇到一些问题，但确实可以解决更新问题（似乎是最新的，所以我认为这不是正常现象吗？）。无论如何，GNOME Web浏览器在将网页“固定”到启动器方面做得很好。这也执行了非常轻量级的沙箱操作，将启动器上的“应用程序”限制为仅几个站点。我非常喜欢这种方法，因为它可以快速访问Lyft，Twitter，Mastodon等移动网站，而无论如何我可能都只是使用了浏览器。
 如果您在用于Pinephone的任何基于Phosh的发行版上使用GNOME Web，也可以使用，但是在Librem 5上这样做似乎更令人愉快，即使是因为计算能力的提高。一般要点是，目前无论是手机（还是发行版）都很少有本机应用程序，但是使用移动网站似乎运行良好。
  遗憾的是，这两款手机都没有Bandcamp或Spotify同类产品。 UBPort拥有一个名为Futify的Spotify客户端，该客户端仍处于测试阶段，而不位于OpenStore上（您必须手动获取click包）。但是，当将一对耳机插入手机上的3.5mm插孔时，Ubuntu touch也无法处理切换为耳机的问题。
 Librem 5确实可以处理切换到耳机的操作，但是缺少流媒体应用程序有点麻烦。同样，似乎Librem 5不会自动安装/检测我的FAT格式的SD卡。
 不用说，您可以在这些电话上听音乐，并且耳机插孔是非常非常受欢迎的功能，但是您需要做一些工作才能进入这里。
 另外，对于Pinephone和Librem 5（基于Phosh的发行版），我强烈建议使用Lollypop。它非常注重通过“专辑”进行分类/播放，而不是单独播放歌曲，但是它是一款非常令人愉快的应用。这个应用程序显然已经有很多想法。
  为Librem 5充电似乎需要永远。不完全是。在官方Matrix会议室（＃community-librem-5：talk.puri.sm）中询问后，似乎正在对此进行处理。总体结论：USB-C是最糟糕的，而电源却很难。我倾向于在这两点上达成共识，但一点令人失望的是，使用手机随附的充电器和电缆以外的任何其他方式进行充电现在有点麻烦。希望将来的软件更新能够解决此问题。 
在Pinephone方面，我读到电池充电可能会危害生命。这不仅仅是个令人担忧的问题。但是，很显然，这些设备还没有作为Android的替代品销售。 myPinephone并没有发生任何不良情况，但值得关注。
  这两款手机的运行温度都很高。我认为，考虑到我握着手机的方式（更靠近手机底座），我发现Librem 5的频率要比我注意到Pinephone的温度更高。这主要是因为Librem 5的框架/底盘是金属的，并在手机侧面传导热量。因此，使用手机一段时间后，您会发现手机变热。
 总而言之，如果您感觉到Pinephone上的CPU在哪里，那么在高负载下使用手机时温度的感受就不会有太大不同。这并不意味着温度没有变化，但是我还没有足够的措施来说两种方式。
  啊哈！您以为我不会谈论手机的形状和大小。嗯，这是：
 Pinephone的形状像电话。它的宽度有点高，让人难以忘怀，特别是考虑到屏幕分辨率远低于例如屏幕分辨率。我的Pixel3，但它是手机形状的。它非常适合我的口袋，否则就像2020年的智能手机一样。
 库5，但是。好吧，这是一个问题。这东西太厚了。它的厚度大约是我的Pixel 3的两倍，而且不是全电池。它的形状更像是外部硬盘驱动器，而不是电话。这肯定从任何口袋里鼓出来，有点大。它也很沉重，与myPixel或Pinephone相比，握持更长的时间要累得多。从美学的角度来看，它就像一块巨大的砖头。
 我知道我在这里大量使用Librem 5，但是不要指望iPhone级别的价格标签意味着您将获得iPhone级别的设备。请注意：如果您希望这款手机“比看起来更薄”，您将感到失望。 
根据我的经验，Pine64（对于所有设备，不仅是Pinephone）是一个充满活力和积极的社区。 Pine64在其社区更新，透明度以及他们如何协作以及如何回馈给为其手机供电的项目方面展现出的领导力是支持该项目的强烈理由。我总是喜欢阅读每月更新，即使我知道我很快就不会再购买任何Pine设备。
 我也特别感谢他们如何从每次“ Pinephone社区版”运行中向这些社区组织背后的项目捐款。这是帮助赞助和促进FOSS的好方法，我很高兴这成为可能。
 我真的不知道关于Pine64可以说的本来就是可怕的事情。他们在做自己的工作方面做得非常出色。
 附言他们的网上商店过去很烂，但是变得更好了。但是，创建一个网店非常困难，因此，他们能够像以前一样管理网店。 FOSS Shopify在您需要的时候在哪里？
  纯粹主义

note_en:
Huzzah! I recently received my Librem 5 (Evergreen) from Purism. The Librem 5 is a smartphone that runs an otherwisestandard linux kernel. However, unlike Android which also relies on the linuxkernel under the hood, the Librem 5 uses a GNU userspace, adapted for mobile.This makes it more akin to your typical laptop in some ways, although the formfactor still resembles a modern smartphone (at least, mostly). Here are somepreliminary thoughts about the phone and how it compares to Pine64’s Pinephone, which is another phone that usesneither Android nor iOS, and relies on a GNU / Linux based OS.
 This is a bit of a long post, and I’m sorry for that. Brevity is certainly ahard-earned skill, but going forward without comparing the Librem 5 to thePinephone seemed like the wrong choice ;)
   Purism touts the Librem 5 as a “Security and Privacy Focused Phone.” This isnot wholly untrue, and there are some features that are unique to the Librem 5in this respect. For example, there are “kill-switches” on one side of thephone that allow you to disable:
  There’s an additional feature where if you disable all three switches, thenother sensors on the phone such as GPS are all disabled as well. You’d bebetter served reading through the  Librem 5 productpage to learn more.
  In contrast to the Librem 5, which touts itself as being privacy and securityfocused, the  Pinephone is touted as “AnOpen Source Smart Phone Supported by All Major Linux Phone Projects.” It isconsiderably different from the Librem 5 in many ways, from the hardware allthe way to the final software. It too has kill-switches, but these are not easyto access on the side of the phone and instead are inside the case of thephone. You can access these kill-switches by removing the back plate.
 One of the neat things about the Pinephone is the ability to boot and use avariety of Linux distributions on the hardware itself. It’s as easy as loadingthe distro onto an SD card, and then booting the phone. You don’t even have toflash anything to the internal eMMC in order to run it!
   The Librem 5 starts at $799.00 USD, or can be purchased at $1999.00 USD if youwant your Librem 5 assembled in the good ol’ US of A before they ship it toyou. There’s a lot of talk about anti-interdiction or controlling the supplychain, but trust me in saying that you’re definitely not in the market for a$2k phone like the Librem 5. I don’t know anyone who is.
 I could probably write an entire post on the economics of this, but quitefrankly, it’s kind of appalling that literally anyone would pay this much. It’skind of appalling that Purism would charge this much. I can imagine a schemewhere the parts are shipped from China, and verified and assembled by apart-time college student in the States and they’d likely still be able toprice it cheaper than $1999.00.
 Anyways this post isn’t about Purism’s business practices, of which I willcomment on at some point, but regardless I will drop this matter here. Pleasejust don’t spend $1999.00 on a Librem 5.
 And for that one person who will eventually claim they did purchase the$1999.00 USA version and it was very necessary for their use case:  look buddy,I get it, you’ve drank the Kool-Aid on this one but I’m sincerely doubtful thatyour very specific concerns and use-case apply to even the general nerd whogets excited about the intersection of FOSS and mobile hardware. Moreover, I’mdoubtful that even if they did apply to such a niche group that $2k is a pricepoint upon which you still don’t balk at and go look for literally anythingelse.
 I bought my Librem 5 back during the original crowdfunding campaign, so I endedup spending a lot less than even the $799 USD. Surprisingly, I’ve been waitingon this phone since around the end of 2017, and here we are in 2020 and Ifinally have it. Purism has been sending out what are effectively  DVT andEVT runs of thephone that have been going through several phases named Aspen, Birch, Chestnut,Dogwood, and finally Evergreen. Evergreen is supposed to be the final versionof the hardware, which I opted for. I wanted to get the final, true Librem 5,not one that could be radically different. While my unit does not have FCCcertification (it is technically what you might consider a PVT unit), it isrepresentative of the final hardware that Purism will land on for this phone,as there is ostensibly no more hardware revisions.
  The Pinephone starts at $149.99 USD, sold in small batches surrounding aparticular distro / community that is building software for the device (called <distro-name> Community Edition). Recent batches have made a “convergenceedition” available, for $199.99 USD. The convergence editions have an extragigabyte of RAM and a larger eMMC flash storage, and are touted as being usefulfor connecting the Pinephone to a full-size monitor and running it as if youhad a full desktop available.
 I originally purchased a Pinephone from the UBPorts CE batch. Eventually, Iupgraded my mainboard so that my specs match that of the convergence editionsfrom later batches. All in all, I’ve managed to spend more than the original$200, but I’m not disappointed, since I have had a lot of fun with thePinephone.
  For the vast majority of people, the Librem 5 and Pinephone are probably notworth even considering. The GNU userspace and associated “mobile” paradigmswithin it are very much not ready for daily use.
 For the Librem 5, there are some security aspects that this “security andprivacy focused phone” don’t even hit (e.g. hardware backed security / secureenclave, device encryption, etc.). That’s not to say that these can’t be madepossible in future updates, but as far as the software story today goes, theydo not yet exist.
  Anyways, so why do I, the author, care? I will admit, after my first bout ofusage, neither phone will be a daily driver for me. I will probably continue tostick to my Pixel 3, and it is very likely that I will eventually get anotherAndroid device at some point in the next two years. Linux phones are definitelynot yet ready to be daily drivers; however, they are interesting for a few keyreasons.
  Digital waste is a blight on our society. I can admit that the year-over-yearhardware cycle provides a vast set of improvements that can change thesmartphone landscape. However, I also acknowledge that the number of phones,tablets, and other electronics that find their way into landfills or areotherwise rendered into trash is a growing concern. Linux phones and mobilehardware present an opportunity to move away from that, and it’s important tosupport this in some way.
  I’m a fan of free software! While my day job isn’t working on FOSS, I thinkthat there’s a lot of benefits to having better FOSS software available. Theleast of which is because leaps in the mobile space have also attracted othersto work on Linux in the desktop space as well.
  I care about Linux phones because they present a third option from the Android/ iOS duopoly. Even if they suck now in (a lot of) ways, they will improve overtime. I want to move towards a future where I don’t have to choose between anOS that’s built towards data collection and advertising, and another that’sbuilt on locking you into an ecosystem.
  First, before I get into my impressions comparing the two, I should at the veryleast mention what I am / am not testing. I did not / will not test:
 Phone calls & SMS / MMS functionality  I do not have a secondary mobile number, and my primary mobile number isregistered to an e-SIM on my Pixel 3. So the best I can do is test howmobile data works on a data-only SIM.
  Convergence features  I don’t really plan to use these as anything other than a mobile device, soI haven’t bothered plugging them into a display. Additionally, my displaymay be a bit too large and high-resolution for either phone to drive iteffectively.
  Email  This one is still on my TODO list, however I’ve been too lazy to trysetting this up on several of the Pinephone’s possible distros.
  Cameras  The Librem 5 does not yet have a functioning camera. For a phone to shipwith the hardware but no software… Well it’s not typical. So I can’tcompare anything, given that the Librem 5 hasn’t yet made it possible to doso.
  GPS / location  Look, I want to do this more than anybody, but with COVID and all I havebarely needed to leave the house anywhere that navigation would benecessary. So I’m very much only comparing surface features on this front.
  In general, I find Phosh to be awkward to use. I don’t like it. It feelsstilted and static. This is most easily seen in how one launches the app draweror notification tray. Rather than swiping these (which feels exceedinglynatural), you merely tap them. This feels as if it was done this way becausebutton-like behaviour is easier than handling accelerations and swiping. Thisis probably largely personal preference, but I really, really dislike Phosh asa base. The Librem 5 doesn’t have as many UI alternatives as the Pinephone,largely in part due to the facts that:
  So unfortunately for me, I’ll have to stick with Phosh on the Librem 5 for thetime being. This is very much a preference problem, but it is a problem.
 Lomiri is the name of the UI used by  UBPorts / Ubuntutouch (and hopefully one day the Manjaro!!!) distro for the Pinephone. This UIfeels much, much more natural, and I really appreciate the swipe-basedbehaviour of everything. Even compared to modern Android and all its quirks andfeatures I think Lomiri has the right idea for how one might interact withtheir device.
 Despite my preference for Lomiri over Phosh as a phone interface, I do have tosay that it is very apparent the difference in processing power between the twodevices. This is expected, since the Pinephone is vastly cheaper, but theLibrem 5 is solid with regards to how it handles touch inputs, scrolling speed,delays, etc. Lomiri does a good job and not feeling slow on the Pinephone;however, the Librem 5 just feel consistently faster. Better hardware and all,but the software story doesn’t seem like it’s starting from a bad place(granted, this could be very different from anything you read pre-Evergreen,but forward progress is being made here).
 One can certainly hope that one day there will be a Lomiri frontend for theLibrem 5. I am not holding my breath though, since it doesn’t seem as if thereare a lot of distros that are advertising that they want to support the Librem5 (again, probably mostly due to price). I find it a bit of a shame thatthey’ve stuck with Phosh, but hey, Purism went ahead and wrote that themselves,so why wouldn’t they? I do wish something like Ubuntu Touch was availablethough, I could see the software story being very different if that was thecase.
  The on-screen keyboard on the Librem 5 is okay and gets the job done, butleaves something to be desired. Most Pinephone distros also use the samekeyboard, so there isn’t a big winner here. However, Ubuntu Touch’s keyboarddefinitely comes out on top. The fact that it has spelling corrections, wordsuggestions, etc. help make it that much better.
 Again, this is very much a preference thing, and I hope that the keyboardreceives more updates in the future. I don’t see any fundamental reason why itcan’t, or why more advanced features won’t be made possible.
 It may also be worth noting that if you know nothing of these devices: swipingto type is not a feature of any keyboard on Linux phones. Maybe one day, butno, not now.
  One thing that has frustrated me about the Pinephone is that the kill switchesseem to be a secondary feature of the phone. What does that mean? Basically:these kill switches aren’t really as nicely integrated in the software as theycould be. I don’t actually care that you have to remove the backplate to get tothem, but I’ve sometimes noticed a tendency for the software to not gracefullyhandle the fact that one of them could be switched off. I’ve said a lot of goodthings about Ubuntu Touch until now, but it really doesn’t like if you disablethe mobile data / baseband switch. Other distros handle this in various ways,but especially with the original mainboard my UBPorts CE Pinephone had Inoticed that disabling the baseband could sometimes render wifi from neverre-connecting after the device has gone into a deep sleep (i.e. screen is offfor a few hours).
 I’m not in the business of debugging this or ascertaining why, but it seemsthat the kill-switches on this phone have not been a day-1 feature. It’s notthe end of the world given that I now work from home every day (so like, what’sa phone for anyways?), however I would appreciate if the Pinephone worked aswell as the Librem 5 in this regard.
 And on that note, the Librem 5 kill switches  appear to work fantastically. Isay “appear” because it is basically impossible to test the camera and mic killswitch (mostly due to the lack of a functioning camera). I have not tested anycommunication apps yet, as I don’t believe there are any that I currently use.When there’s a fully functional Matrix client on both phones, I’ll be happy togo through and test both :-)
 Nonetheless, the baseband and wifi kill switches do exactly what you expectthem to do on the Librem 5.  Further, when turning them off and on again, thedevice and software recover fantastically. Big win here for the Purism folks,as this can’t have been a small feat.
  Not much to say here. A data-only SIM from Google Fi seems to work pretty wellin either phone. I haven’t had any major issues with connecting to a networkwith either the Pinephone or Librem 5.
  There is not a vast ecosystem for apps on Linux phones yet. Ubuntu Touch boaststhe largest app store, but quite frankly a lot of those apps are not really inmy wheelhouse. However, there are some aspects of apps from my firstimpressions that stand out.
  I know I said that I wasn’t gonna discuss GPS stuff, since I’ve barely used it,but my basic first impressions are:
 The Pinephone (with uNav3) is a pleasant experience, but getting that firstlocation lock can take forever. I believe this to be unique to the Pinephonehardware, but I’m unsure if I’m just doing something wrong.
  The Librem 5 seems to default to getting mobile network location. It does geta position lock quickly, but it seems like it’s not using GPS, so appears tobe inaccurate. This was most apparent when trying to use the Lyft web app,and setting my pickup location using “current position.” I’m glad that thebrowser seems to support this functionality, but it needs some refinement.
  The Librem 5 impresses me a lot with it’s web app support. While I initiallyhad some issues with the browser, an update did manage to correct it (seems tobe recent, so I don’t think it’s the norm?). Anyways, the GNOME Web browserdoes a good job of being able to “pin” web pages to your launcher. Thisperforms a very lightweight sandboxing as well, limiting that “app” on yourlauncher to only a few sites. I really like this approach as it enables quickaccess to mobile websites like Lyft, Twitter, Mastodon, etc that I might havejust used the browser for anyways.
 This also works if you use GNOME Web on any Phosh-based distro for thePinephone, but doing this on the Librem 5 seemed to be much more pleasant, ifonly because of the increase in computing power. The general gist is that veryfew native apps currently exist for either phone (or distro, really) but usingmobile websites seems to work pretty well.
  Alas, neither phones have a good Bandcamp or Spotify equivalent yet. UBPortshas a Spotify client called Futify, which is still in beta and not on the OpenStore (you have to get the click package manually). However, Ubuntu touch alsodoesn’t handle switching to headphones when a pair of headphones are pluggedinto the 3.5mm jack on the phone.
 The Librem 5 does appear to handle switching to headphones, but the lack ofstreaming apps is a bit of a pain. Likewise, it seems that my FAT-formatted SDcard is not automatically mounted / detected by the Librem 5.
 Needless to say, you can listen to music on these phones, and the headphonejack is a very, very welcome addition, but you’ll need to do some work to getgoing here.
 Also, for both the Pinephone and Librem 5 (Phosh based distros), I highlysuggest using  Lollypop. It is heavilyfocused on sorting / playing via “albums” as opposed to individual songs, butit’s a very pleasant app to use. A lot of thought has clearly been put intothis app.
  Charging the Librem 5 seems to take forever. No, really. It seems after askingin the official Matrix room ( #community-librem-5:talk.puri.sm) this issomething that is being worked on. General conclusion: USB-C is the worst, andpower is hard. I’m inclined to agree on both those points but it is a littledisappointing that charging using anything other than the charger + cable thatcame with the phone is a bit of a toss-up right now. Future software updatesshould hopefully fix this.
 On the Pinephone side I’ve read that  charging the battery can bedangerous. This is more than a little concerning.However, it’s also pretty clear these devices aren’t being marketed as anAndroid replacement just yet. I haven’t had anything bad happen to myPinephone, but it’s worth keeping an eye out for.
  Both phones can run fairly hot. I think given how I hold the phone (closer tothe base of the phone) I tend to notice the Librem 5 more often than I noticethe Pinephone getting hotter. This is mostly because the frame / chassis forthe Librem 5 is metal, and conducts the heat around the sides of the phone. Soyou’ll notice it getting hot after you’ve used your phone for a good bit.
 All in all though, if you feel where the CPU is on the Pinephone, it’s notvastly different in terms of how the temperature  feels while using the phone onhigh load. That doesn’t mean the temperatures aren’t different, but I haven’tmeasured enough to say either way.
  Aha! You thought I wasn’t going to talk about the shape and size of the phone.Well, here it is:
 The Pinephone is shaped like a phone. It is a little wide and tall for mytastes, especially given the screen resolution is much lower than e.g. my Pixel3, but it is phone shaped. It fits in my pocket well, and otherwise feels likea smartphone does in 2020.
 The Librem 5, however. Well, it’s a chonker, that’s for sure. This thing ismassively thick. It’s about twice the thickness of my Pixel 3, and it’s not allbattery. This is shaped more like an external hard drive than a phone. Thisdefinitely bulges out of any pocket and is somewhat comically large. It’s alsoheavy, and I find it much more tiresome to hold for longer periods than say, myPixel or the Pinephone. From an aesthetic point of view, it just looks like agiant brick.
 I know I’m ragging on the Librem 5 a lot here, however don’t expect that theiPhone-level price tag means you’re getting an iPhone-level device. Be warned:if you are expecting this phone to be “thinner than it seems” you will bedisappointed.
   Pine64 (for all their devices, not just the Pinephone) is really a vibrant andpositive community in my experience. The leadership that Pine64 shows in theircommunity updates, their trasnparency, and how they collaborate and give backto the projects powering their phone are very strong reasons for supporting theproject. I always love reading the monthly updates, even if I know I’m notbuying any more Pine devices anytime soon.
 I also especially appreciate how they have been donating money from everyPinephone Community Edition run to the projects behind those communityeditions. It’s a great way to help sponsor and foster FOSS, and I am so happythat this is made possible.
 I really don’t know if there’s anything I can say about Pine64 that isinherently awful. They’ve done a fantastic job doing what they’re doing.
 P.S. Their web store used to suck but its gotten a lot better. Making a webstore is insanely hard though, so props to them for being able to manage it asthey have been. Where’s the FOSS Shopify when you need it?
  Purism on
......
title: Librem 5 Evergreen与Pinephone

Stay on top of the latest market trends and economic insights with the Axios Markets newsletter. Sign up for free.
 
   The Trump administration will maintain the current standards for soot pollution, rejecting the recommendations of agency scientists to tighten them, the  Wall Street Journal reports.
 The state of play: EPA officials said they decided to keep the 12-micrograms-per-cubic-meter limit after review by a scientific advisory committee and considering public comments, per WSJ.
 The move is "backed by chemical, oil and other industry groups that bear the costs of implementing the higher standards," the Journal writes.
 Critics say low-income and minority communities, which are disproportionately affected by soot, will bear the brunt of the decision.
 Yes, but: The agency's scientists in a report last year said studies support a new limit between 8 and 10 micrograms, which they said could potentially save more than 12,000 lives a year.
 But officials noted that U.S. particulate matter levels are 20% lower than in France, Germany and Great Britain, and five times below the world average, per WSJ.
 What they're saying: “The EPA under the Trump Administration has continued America’s leadership in clear air, lowering our particulate matter levels to well below those of many of our global competitors,” EPA Administrator Andrew Wheeler said in a statement, per WSJ.
 “Maintaining these important standards will ensure Americans can continue to breathe some of the cleanest air on the planet.”
title: EPA拒绝收紧烟尘污染标准
title_en: EPA declines to tighten soot pollution standards

由于所有的高层建筑，要在城市中获得可靠的GPS位置可能很难。摩天大楼会反射GPS信号，并使您的位置数据变得疯狂，但是现在，谷歌表示，它可以对此进行更正并为用户提供更准确的位置。 Google正在努力进行更新，以利用Google Maps ##; 3D建筑物数据以计算建筑物如何干扰GPS，并表示Android的新的3D映射辅助校正模块。可以校正GPS反弹。
 GPS的工作原理快速复习：您的位置由太空中的卫星划分。您的电话从GPS卫星接收信号，该信号包括卫星的位置和非常精确的时间戳。 GPS时间戳告诉电话信号从太空到达您所用的时间，然后您只需乘以光速即可与卫星保持距离。如果您从多颗卫星收到这样的信号，则可以将地球上的位置缩小到几英尺。
 只要您能欣赏开阔的天空，所有这些奇特的空间计算都将非常有用。 GPS三角剖分假设您的信号是从卫星直射到手机的，但是并非总是如此。在城市中，巨大的玻璃和金属摩天大楼可以将GPS信号从太空向下反射。如果您的GPS信号包含跳弹，突然之间，您的＆times x光速＆＃34;方程不等于您到卫星的距离，三角三角形的边长比应有的长，并且GPS坐标不再精确。在城市中，这可能意味着您的位置突然在街道的错误一侧或错误的街区。
    Google制定了一种方案，可以通过校正距离计算中的GPS反弹来使GPS在城市中更好地工作。 Google的Google非常好解决方案是利用Google地图中提供的大量3D建筑数据。 Google并没有详细说明，但是如果GPS从建筑物中弹起，当您知道建筑物在哪里时，您可以绘制反弹距离并进行校正。 Google说，通过使用新的＆＃34; 3D辅助映射更正，＆＃34; ＆＃34;城市中[位置]的准确性得到了显着提高。 Android的定位系统现在可以将路边错误发生率降低大约75％。 Google在画廊中的前后照片非常具有说明性。
    作为3D映射辅助校正模块的新3D GPS校正方案正在Android上推广。这是Google Play服务的一部分融合位置提供者API（FLP）。 FLP是应用在Android上查询位置的首选方式-FLP代表系统，而不是原始GPS坐标（也可通过API调用获得）。尽力而为，同时还能节省电池。启动GPS并进行大量的外层空间计算非常耗电量，因此FLP尝试尽可能少地执行此操作。例如，几乎每部Android手机都为Google的Wi-Fi接入点位置的全球数据库做出了贡献，因此，如果您连接到已知的接入点，则Android无需GPS就能知道您的位置。 FLP充当整个系统的位置提供者，因此FLP可以一次确定您的位置，并与任何具有后台位置许可的应用程序共享，而不必使用五个应用程序来启动GPS并自行计算。
 Google表示，这些功能将在全球3850个城市中使用-基本上所有拥有3D建筑数据的地方都可以使用。 3D建筑物计算可与美国军方拥有的GPS以及全球一些主要的等效系统一起使用：俄罗斯的GLONASS，欧盟的伽利略，日本的QZSS，甚至中国的北斗导航卫星系统，仅对中国游客真正有用，因为在中国销售的手机上无法使用Google Play服务和融合位置提供商。目前，Pixel 5和Pixel 4a（5G）在改进的映射功能上获得了首当其冲的优势，到目前为止，它仅在行走时才起作用。 Google表示，3D映射更正将于2021年初推广到整个Android生态系统（Android 8或更高版本）。
note_en:
Getting a solid GPS location in a city can be tough thanks to all the tall buildings. Skyscrapers reflect the GPS signal and can make your location data go crazy, but now, Google says it can correct for this and give users a much more accurate location. Google is working on  an update that will leverage Google Maps' 3D building data to calculate how buildings interfere with GPS, and it says Android's new "3D mapping aided corrections module" can correct for GPS bounces.
 A quick refresher on how GPS works: your position is triangulated by satellites in space. Your phone receives a signal from a GPS satellite, consisting of the position of the satellite and a very precise timestamp. The GPS time stamp tells the phone how long the signal took to reach you from space, and then you just multiply that by the speed of light to get your distance from the satellite. If you get a signal like this from multiple satellites, you can narrow down your position on Earth to a few feet.
 All of these fancy space calculations work great, provided you have an open view of the sky. GPS triangulation assumes your signals are taking a straight shot from the satellite to your phone, but that's not always the case. In a city, the giant glass-and-metal skyscrapers can reflect the GPS signal on its way down from space. If your GPS signal includes a ricochet, suddenly your "time x speed-of-light" equation doesn't equal your distance from the satellite, a leg of your triangulation triangle is longer than it should be, and your GPS coordinates aren't accurate anymore. In a city, this can mean your location is suddenly on the wrong side of the street—or the wrong block.
    Google has cooked up a scheme to make GPS work better in cities by correcting for the GPS bounces in the distance calculation. Google's very "Google" solution for this is leveraging the massive amounts of 3D building data it has available in Google Maps. Google doesn't go much into the details, but if the GPS is bouncing off of buildings, when you know where the buildings are, you can map out the bounce distance and correct for that. Google says that by using the new "3D aided mapping corrections," "the [location] accuracy in cities improves spectacularly." Android's location system can now "reduce wrong-side-of-street occurrences by approximately 75%." Google's before-and-after picture in the gallery, above, is very illustrative.
    The new 3D GPS-correction scheme is rolling out to Android as the "3D mapping aided corrections module," which is part of the Google Play Services' Fused Location Provider API (FLP). The FLP is the preferred way for apps to query location on Android—rather than raw GPS coordinates (which are also available via an API call), FLP represents the systems' best effort at location while also preserving the battery. Firing up GPS and making a bunch of outer space calculations is very battery intensive, so FLP tries to do that as little as possible. For instance, nearly every Android phone contributes to Google's worldwide database of Wi-Fi access point locations, so if you're connected to a known access point, Android can tell where you are without needing GPS. FLP acts as a location provider for the entire system, so rather than five apps trying to fire up GPS and make their own calculations, FLP can determine your location once and share it with any app that has the background location permission.
 Google says the features will work in "3850 cities around the world"—basically everywhere Google has 3D building data. The 3D building calculations work with GPS, which is owned by the United States military, along with some of the major worldwide equivalents: Russia's GLONASS, the EU's Galileo, Japan's QZSS, and even China's BeiDou Navigation Satellite System, which is only really good for visitors to China since Google Play Services and the Fused Location Provider is not available on phones sold in China. For now, the Pixel 5 and Pixel 4a (5G) get first dibs on the improved mapping feature, and so far, it only works while walking. Google says the 3D mapping corrections will rollout to "the entire Android ecosystem (Android 8 or later) in early 2021."
title: Google承诺通过3D建筑数据“改善”城市GPS
title_en: Google promises “spectacular” city GPS improvement with 3D building data

note:
早在12月8日星期二，SpaceX团队将首次从我们位于德克萨斯州卡梅伦县的站点对星舰8号（SN8）进行高空亚轨道飞行测试。与所有开发测试一样，进度表是动态的，并且可能会发生变化。
  这次亚轨道飞行旨在测试多个目标，从车辆的三个Raptor发动机的性能如何到车辆的整体空气动力学进入能力（包括其车身襟翼），到车辆如何管理推进剂过渡。 SN8还将尝试执行着陆翻转机动，这将是这种规模车辆的首次尝试。
  通过这样的测试，成功不是通过特定目标的完成来衡量的，而是我们可以学到多少，这将随着SpaceX迅速推进星际飞船的发展而告知并提高未来成功的可能性。
  仅在过去的一年中，SpaceX已使用Starship SN5和SN6完成了两次低空飞行测试，并在330台地面发动机启动期间累积了超过16,000秒的运行时间，其中包括多次Starship静态起火以及可重复使用的美他乐全流阶段飞行测试燃烧猛禽发动机。此外，随着生产的加速和保真度的提高，SpaceX已建造了10个Starship原型。 SN9几乎已准备就绪，可以使用该板，该板现在具有两个用于快速开发测试的有效支架。
  SN8的飞行测试是开发完全可重复使用的运输系统的令人振奋的下一步，该系统能够将机组人员和货物运送到地球轨道，月球，火星及其他地方。当我们冒险进入新的领域时，我们将继续感谢收到的所有支持和鼓励。
  这里将提供飞行测试的现场直播，该直播将在起飞前几分钟开始。鉴于时间表的不确定性，请继续关注我们的社交媒体频道，以进行最新的《星际飞船》高空飞行测试！
note_en:
As early as Tuesday, December 8, the SpaceX team will make the first attempt of a high-altitude suborbital flight test of Starship serial number 8 (SN8) from our site in Cameron County, Texas. The schedule is dynamic and likely to change, as is the case with all development testing.
  This suborbital flight is designed to test a number of objectives, from how the vehicle’s three Raptor engines perform to the overall aerodynamic entry capabilities of the vehicle (including its body flaps) to how the vehicle manages propellant transition. SN8 will also attempt to perform a landing flip maneuver, which would be a first for a vehicle of this size.
  With a test such as this, success is not measured by completion of specific objectives but rather how much we can learn, which will inform and improve the probability of success in the future as SpaceX rapidly advances development of Starship.
  This past year alone, SpaceX has completed two low-altitude flight tests with Starship SN5 and SN6 and accumulated over 16,000 seconds of run time during 330 ground engine starts, including multiple Starship static fires and four flight tests of the reusable methalox full-flow staged combustion Raptor engine. Additionally, with production accelerating and fidelity increasing, SpaceX has built 10 Starship prototypes. SN9 is almost ready to move to the pad, which now has two active stands for rapid development testing.
  SN8’s flight test is an exciting next step in the development of a fully reusable transportation system capable of carrying both crew and cargo to Earth orbit, the Moon, Mars, and beyond. As we venture into new territory, we continue to appreciate all of the support and encouragement we have received.
  There will be a live feed of the flight test available here that will start a few minutes prior to liftoff. Given the uncertainty of the schedule, stay tuned to our social media channels for updates as we move toward our first high-altitude flight test of Starship!
title: 即将进行的测试：SpaceX Starship高空飞行
title_en: Upcoming test: SpaceX Starship high-altitude flight

移动优先隐私项目MobileCoin自2017年首次发行代币（ICO）以来一直有点白鲸，该公司周一宣布启动主网。
 尽管如此，细节仍然很少。 MobileCoin基金会在简短的媒体帖子中写道，该项目不适用于美国用户或“其他受禁管辖区中的个人或实体”。
  但是，该项目的GitHub存储库仍将MobileCoin描述为testnet。 MobileCoin的网站也是如此。也就是说，该资产已在FTX交易所上市，其创始人Sam Samman-Fried在一封电子邮件中向CoinDesk确认，该电子邮件是“可提取的主网MobileCoin”。
 通过注册，您将收到有关CoinDesk产品的电子邮件，并且您同意我们的条款＆amp;条件和隐私政策。
 由私人消息应用程序Signal的创建者Moxie Marlinspike建议，MobileCoin是最著名的加密货币项目。 Marlinspike的参与首先在2017年12月在Wired上报道了该项目及其创始人Joshua Goldbard的故事。
 “这两个基于开源恒星共识协议平台，” Wired当时报道。
 2018年5月，根据简单的未来代币协议（SAFT），MobileCoin通过美国证券交易委员会的文件宣布了3000万美元的融资。 
去年9月，微软在博客文章中谈到了MobileCoin，描述了该公司如何利用科技巨头的Azure技术对数据进行加密，即使该数据在使用时也被称为“机密计算”。
 MobileCoin基金会网站列出了多个组织作为成员，其中大多数是非营利组织：Long Now基金会，社区艺术稳定基金会，超越国界的想法和为未来而战。它还列出了存储公司Dreamhost和区块链基础设施平台Blockdaemon。
 MobileCoin的Goldbard并未立即回复CoinDesk的置评请求。
 MobileCoin不是唯一一家尝试为移动支付带来快速便捷支付的公司。 Sirin Labs发生故障的Finney电话也应该这样做。 Telegram在为其Telegram开放网络筹集了17亿美元之后，尝试了类似的项目。在受到美国证券监管机构的压制之后，Telegram在5月结束了其加密项目。
 MobileCoin的发布也许可以解释为什么通常沉默寡言的Marlinspike愿意在今年秋天与The New Yorker进行相当广泛的讨论，尽管这个故事从未提及MobileCoin或加密货币。
   披露CoinDesk是区块链新闻的领导者，是一家致力于追求最高新闻标准并遵守一套严格的编辑政策的媒体机构。 CoinDesk是Digital Currency Group的独立运营子公司，该公司投资于加密货币和区块链初创公司。
note_en:
Mobile-first privacy project MobileCoin, which has been a bit of a white whale since the initial coin offering (ICO) era of 2017, announced a mainnet launch on Monday.
 Still, details remain scant. The MobileCoin Foundation  wrote in a brief Medium post that the project is not available to U.S. users or “persons or entities in other prohibited jurisdictions.”
  The project’s  GitHub repository still describes MobileCoin as in testnet, however. The same is true for  MobileCoin’s website. That said, the asset is listed on  the FTX exchange with founder Sam Bankman-Fried confirming to CoinDesk in an email that “it’s withdrawable mainnet MobileCoin.”
 By signing up, you will receive emails about CoinDesk products and you agree to our  terms & conditions and  privacy policy.
 MobileCoin is best known as the cryptocurrency project advised by Moxie Marlinspike, the creator of private messaging app Signal. Marlinspike’s involvement was first revealed in a December 2017 story in  Wired on the project and its founder, Joshua Goldbard.
 “The two based it on the open-source Stellar Consensus Protocols platform,” Wired reported at the time.
 In May 2018, MobileCoin announced a $30 million round via a  Securities and Exchange Commission filing, under a simple agreement for future tokens (SAFT).
   In September, Microsoft wrote about MobileCoin in  a blog post, describing how it used the tech giant’s Azure technology to encrypt data even while it is being used, which Microsoft refers to as “confidential computing.”
 The  MobileCoin Foundation website lists several organizations as members, most of which are nonprofits: The Long Now Foundation, Community Arts Stabilization Trust, Ideas Beyond Borders and Fight for the Future. It also lists storage company  Dreamhost and blockchain infrastructure platform  Blockdaemon.
 MobileCoin’s Goldbard did not immediately reply to a request for comment from CoinDesk.
 MobileCoin is not the only company to attempt to bring fast and easy payments to mobile. The failed  Finney phone from Sirin Labs was meant to do the same. Telegram attempted a similar project after raising $1.7 billion for its Telegram Open Network. Telegram ended its crypto project  in May following pushback from U.S. securities regulators.
 The MobileCoin launch may explain why the typically reticent Marlinspike was willing to participate in a fairly wide-ranging profile with  The New Yorker this autumn, though the story never mentions MobileCoin or cryptocurrency.
   Disclosure The leader in blockchain news, CoinDesk is a media outlet that strives for the highest journalistic standards and abides by a  strict set of editorial policies. CoinDesk is an independent operating subsidiary of  Digital Currency Group, which invests in  cryptocurrencies and blockchain  startups.
title: Signal Founder建议的加密货币上线，开始在FTX上交易
title_en: Cryptocurrency Advised by Signal Founder Goes Live, Begins Trading on FTX

note:
参考底图的前提是可以从称为断言的一系列分布式事务中构造知识图。多个断言通过称为归约的过程进行组合，可以使用集合将其归为有用的分组。
 参考底图中数据的基本单位。可以指定其出处并经过加密签名的不可变语句，以允许基于信任和基于上下文的筛选。
 接受一组断言并将其合并以创建一致状态的过程。减少允许将断言用作更改较大图形的不可变事务。
 能够管理一组有用范围的图形数据的容器。可以对集合进行版本控制，并包括一组断言，简化模式，其他子集合和元数据。
 托管服务，用于管理集合并实施使用策略。注册表可以实现查询功能，用户身份验证或其他使内容及其创建更易于访问的功能。
 正在与协议和规范并行开发第一个Underlay注册中心R1。
note_en:
The Underlay is premised on the idea that a knowledge graph can be constructed from a series of distributed transactions called  assertions. Multiple assertions are combined through a process called  reduction and can be curated into useful groupings using  collections.
 The fundamental unit of data in the Underlay. Immutable statements that can specify their provenance and be cryptographically signed to allow for trust- and context-based filtering.
 The process of taking a set of assertions and merging them to create a consistent state. Reduction allows assertions to be used as immutable transactions that change a larger graph.
 Containers that enable curation of a usefully scoped set of graph data. Collections can be versioned and include a set of assertions, a reduction schema, other sub-collections, and metadata.
 A hosted service that manages collections and implements usage policies. Registries can implement query capabilities, user authentication, or other features that make the content and its creation more accessible.
 A first Underlay registry, R1, is being developed in parallel to the protocol and specs.
title: 参考底图-世界上的分布式知识图
title_en: Underlay – The world’s distributed knowledge graph

note:
上周的某个时候，盗贼从MSI在中国大陆的工厂偷走了大量基于Nvidia的RTX 3090图形卡。该新闻来自Twitter用户@ GoFlying8，他今天早上在盗窃案中发布了似乎是MSI官方内部文件的内容，以及一个中文网站的评论。 MSI文档经过粗略翻译（换句话说，经过OCR扫描，通过Google Translate运行，并且真正被您切掉了最讨厌的边缘），MSI文档的内容如下：
 Ensmai Electronics（Deep）Co.，Ltd.公告备忘录1-20-12-4-000074主题：关于图形卡的报告失窃，应予以奖励
  最近，该公司生产的高单价显示卡被罪犯盗走了。此案现已报警方。同时，我也希望公司全体员工积极，如实地举报本案。
 提供解决这一问题的信息的人将获得10万元的奖励。公司承诺对举报人的身份严格保密。
 如果有任何人参与此案，则自公告之日起，向公司审计部门或冲突部门负责人报告。如果举报属实，并有助于追回失踪物品，公司将向警方举报，但要求宽大处理。法律应得到认真处理。
 有了这个公告，我敦促我的同事们要专业和道德，要有纪律，从案例中学习并受到警告。
 英语技术媒体上的盗窃案引起了一些混乱。 MSI文件本身的日期为上周五，没有详细说明有多少张卡被盗或总价值是多少。周围的评论（似乎是一个中国新闻应用程序）声称，盗窃了大约40个集装箱的RTX 3090卡，总价值约为220万元人民币（合33.6万美元）。 
如果报告的值正确（并参考卡的建议零售价），那么我们正在谈论大约224个被盗的GPU。 由于供应不足且需求非常高，这些卡的售价实际上可能更高。据报道，在eBay上，黄牛对单个RTX 3090的合法出价高达20,000美元，而典型的拍卖价格则为2,600美元。
note_en:
Some time last week, thieves stole a large number of Nvidia-based  RTX 3090 graphics cards from MSI's factory in mainland China. The news comes from twitter user @GoFlying8, who  posted what appears to be an official MSI internal document around the theft this morning, along with commentary from a Chinese language website. Roughly translated—in other words, OCR scanned, run through Google Translate, and with the nastiest edges sawn off by yours truly—the MSI document reads something like this:
 Ensmai Electronics (Deep) Co., Ltd. Announcement Memo No. 1-20-12-4-000074 Subject: Regarding the report theft of the graphics card, it is appropriate to reward
  Recently, high unit price display cards produced by the company have been stolen by criminals. The case has now been reported to the police. At the same time, I also hope that all employees of the company will actively and truthfully report this case.
 Anyone providing information which solves this case will receive a reward of 100,000 yuan. The company promises to keep the identity of the whistleblower strictly confidential.
 If any person is involved in the case, from the date of the public announcement, report to the company's audit department or the head of the conflicting department. If the report is truthful and and assists in the recovery of the missing items, the company will report to the police, but request leniency. The law should be dealt with seriously.
 With this announcement, I urge my colleagues to be professional and ethical, and to be disciplined, learn from cases, and be warned.
 There has been some confusion surrounding the theft in English speaking tech media; the MSI document itself dates to last Friday, and does not detail how many cards were stolen or what the total value was. The surrounding commentary—from what seems to be a Chinese news app—claims that the theft was about 40 containers of RTX 3090 cards, at a total value of about 2.2 million renminbi ($336K in US dollars).
    If the reported value is correct—and refers to suggested retail price of the cards—we're talking about roughly 224 stolen GPUs. The cards might actually sell for considerably more, since supply is low and demand is very high—scalpers on eBay have reportedly gotten some  legitimate bids of as high as $20,000 for a single RTX 3090, with  typical auctions going for more like $2,600.
title: 价值340,000美元的Nvidia RTX 3090显卡在中国被盗
title_en: $340,000 of Nvidia RTX 3090 graphics cards were stolen in China

note:
优步周一在一份声明中说，优步已经在自动驾驶汽车项目上花费了数亿美元，高管们曾经认为这是实现盈利的关键，该公司正在将自动驾驶汽车的工作移交给硅谷的一家初创公司。
 优步还将向这家名为Aurora的初创公司投资4亿美元，因此，它实际上是在向该公司付款，以接管自动驾驶汽车业务，这已成为财务和法律上的头疼事。 Uber可能会许可Aurora设法创造的任何技术。
 这项交易意味着高调但最终却大打折扣，用可自行驾驶的机器取代Uber的人工驾驶。这也预示着其他自动驾驶汽车项目所面临的挑战，这些项目已经从硅谷和汽车制造商那里获得了数十亿美元的投资，但到目前为止还没有生产出一些机器人汽车，有人认为这会在大街上。
 Aurora首席执行官Chris Urmson表示，Aurora的首款产品不会是可以帮助Uber的打车业务的自动出租车。取而代之的是，它可能会是自动驾驶卡车，乌尔姆森认为，由于在公路上驾驶的长途卡车更可预测，而且不涉及乘客，因此乌姆森认为，短期内成功的机会更大。
 优步首席执行官Dara Khosrowshahi在一份声明中表示，他期待“在未来几年内”将Aurora技术推向市场。优步拒绝对该协议进一步置评。
 在自动驾驶汽车项目中，Uber的努力导致亚利桑那州一名行人死亡。来自Waymo的诉讼，该公司是与Google相同的母公司拥有的自动驾驶汽车公司；尤伯（Uber）前高管被控窃取知识产权的认罪尤其令人担忧。
  它始于一个雄心勃勃的想法，但事后看来却是虚构的：Uber最大的损失是它的司机。如果能够用机器代替驱动程序，最终将减少开支并实现盈利，从而终结数年甚至数十亿的损失。 
优步（Uber）于2015年左右宣布与卡内基·梅隆大学（Carnegie Mellon University）的国家机器人中心建立合作伙伴关系，开始从事自动驾驶汽车的研究。后来，优步从卡内基·梅隆（Carnegie Mellon）挖走了40名工程师和科学家，为其自动驾驶汽车部门（即优步先进技术集团或A.T.G.
 2016年，Uber收购了由前Google工程师Anthony Levandowski领导的自动驾驶卡车初创公司Otto。十年前，当Google首次在公共道路上对其自动驾驶汽车进行测试时，在共同创立Aurora之前曾在Google最初的自动驾驶汽车项目中发挥关键作用的Levandowski先生和Urmson先生是该项目的两个关键工程师。测试是一个里程碑，最终导致了投资狂潮。
 此次收购对Otto的估值为6.8亿美元，当Waymo在2017年初对Uber提起诉讼时称，Levandowski先生窃取了Google的商业机密后，情况变得糟透了。 Uber解雇了Levandowski先生，尽管此案正在审理中，但两家公司最终达成和解。优步（Uber）同意以价值7200万美元的价格向Google提供股份。
 联邦检察官指控Levandowski先生犯有33起盗窃罪，并试图从Google盗窃商业机密。今年初，他被判入狱18个月。
 Uber和其他自动驾驶汽车项目在2018年3月在亚利桑那州坦佩（Tempe）丧生，最终归因于驾驶员失误和其他安全缺陷，这是一个严峻的转折点。优步和其他公司暂停了路试。即使在恢复测试之后，新的投资也会放缓。当Uber最终将汽车退回公路时，他们仍然没有达到预期。
 2018年，Uber从丰田汽车获得了5亿美元的投资，以帮助保持自动驾驶部门的生计。这项投资标志着优步战略的转变，表明该公司不再希望开发自己的自动驾驶汽车，而是将其导航技术插入到传统制造商生产的汽车中。
 一年后，丰田汽车与电装和软银愿景基金合作，增加了投资，向优步的自动驾驶业务投入了10亿美元。该交易对Uber的A.T.G.为72.5亿美元。现金注入购买了A.T.G.一段时间，但其在Uber的长期前景仍不清楚。该部门的运营成本很高，尽管Uber认为它将在2021年实现盈利。 
2019年，一家知名初创公司将自己出售给苹果时，新兴的自动驾驶汽车行业开始整合。
 冠状病毒大流行带来了更多问题。它减少了测试，因为与社会保持距离的规则阻止了公司禁止多名驾驶员驾车以防事故。一些行业资深人士承认，成功比他们预期的要遥远。
 这种大流行还给优步带来了财务压力，随着人们停止旅行，优步的核心打车业务下降了。长期以来，投资者一直在向霍斯罗萨西（Khosrowshahi）施加压力，要求其出售自动驾驶部门。随着Uber收入暴跌，需求变得更加紧迫。
  Aurora的总部位于硅谷，但在匹兹堡设有办事处，其首席技术官卡内基梅隆大学教授，前Uber员工Drew Bagnell在这里工作。除了获得Uber的自动驾驶技术外，它还获得了工程师和支持人员，其中大多数人位于匹兹堡。
 作为两家公司之间协议的一部分，Uber将持有这家初创公司26％的股份，而Khosrowshahi先生将担任Aurora董事会的一员。
 “ Uber仍然可以参与其中，而不必花很多钱在ATG上，但是当这项技术在几年内可以实际部署时，仍然有一条进入市场的道路，” Elliot Katz，co -Phantom Auto的创始人兼首席业务官，该公司是一家帮助公司远程控制自动驾驶汽车的初创公司。
 2018年，Aurora同意向全球最大的两家汽车公司大众汽车集团和现代汽车提供自动驾驶技术。但是，在更广泛的行业动荡中，大众汽车和现代汽车之后都接受了其他合作伙伴，而大众汽车不再与Aurora合作。 
乌尔姆森先生几个月来一直表示，他的初创公司的第一个产品将涉及长途货运。 他说，与优步的交易不会改变这一点。  他说：“如果您是坐在汽车后座的乘客，而驾驶员过于谨慎，那可能会令人沮丧；而如果他们不够谨慎，那就很恐怖了。” “如果你在卡车后面放一卷厕纸，那是另一回事。”
note_en:
Uber, which spent hundreds of millions of dollars on a self-driving car project that executives once believed was a key to becoming profitable, is handing the autonomous vehicle effort over to a Silicon Valley start-up, the companies said on Monday.
 Uber will also invest $400 million in the start-up, called Aurora, so it is essentially paying the company to take over the autonomous car operation, which had become a financial and legal headache. Uber is likely to license whatever technology Aurora manages to create.
 The deal amounts to a fire-sale end to a high-profile but star-crossed effort to replace Uber’s human drivers with machines that could drive on their own. It is also indicative of the challenges facing other autonomous vehicle projects, which have received billions in investments from Silicon Valley and automakers but have not produced the fleets of robotic vehicles some thought would be on the streets by now.
 Aurora’s chief executive, Chris Urmson, said Aurora’s first product will not be a robot taxi that could help with Uber’s ride-hailing business. Instead, it will likely be a self-driving truck, which Mr. Urmson believes has a better chance of success in the near term because long-haul truck driving on highways is more predictable and does not involve passengers.
 In a statement, the Uber chief executive, Dara Khosrowshahi, said he was looking forward to bringing Aurora technology to market “in the years ahead.” Uber declined to comment further on the agreement.
 Among self-driving car projects, Uber’s effort, which led to the death of a  pedestrian in Arizona; a  lawsuit from Waymo, the self-driving car company owned by the same parent company as Google; and a  guilty plea from a former Uber executive accused of stealing intellectual property, was particularly fraught.
  It began with an ambitious — but in hindsight fanciful — proposition: Uber’s biggest expense was its drivers. If it could replace the drivers with machines, it could eventually reduce expenses and become profitable, ending years and billions of losses.
 Uber began its work on autonomous vehicles around 2015 when it announced a partnership with Carnegie Mellon University’s National Robotics Center. Uber  poached 40 engineers and scientists from Carnegie Mellon later, establishing the foundation for its autonomous vehicle unit, called the Uber Advanced Technologies Group, or A.T.G.
 In 2016, Uber acquired the autonomous trucking start-up Otto, led by Anthony Levandowski, a former Google engineer. When Google first tested its self-driving car on public roads a decade ago, Mr. Levandowski and Mr. Urmson, who had played a key role in Google’s original self-driving car project before co-founding Aurora, were two of the project’s key engineers. The test was a milestone that eventually led to an investment frenzy.
 The acquisition, which  valued Otto at $680 million, turned sour when Waymo filed a lawsuit against Uber in early 2017, claiming that Mr. Levandowski had stolen trade secrets from Google. Uber  fired Mr. Levandowski, and though the case went to trial, the companies eventually  settled. Uber agreed to give Google a stake in the company valued at $72 million.
 Federal prosecutors charged Mr. Levandowski with 33 counts of theft and attempted theft of trade secrets from Google. He was sentenced to 18 months in prison earlier this year.
 The death of a woman in Tempe, Ariz., in March 2018 —  ultimately attributed to driver error and other safety shortcomings — was a grim turning point for Uber and other self-driving car projects. Uber and  other companies  suspended their road tests. Even after tests resumed, new investments slowed. When Uber finally returned its cars to the road, they continued to  fall short of expectations.
 In 2018, Uber received a  $500 million investment from Toyota to help keep the self-driving unit afloat. The investment marked a shift in Uber’s strategy, signaling that the company no longer wanted to develop its own self-driving car but rather plug its navigation technology into a vehicle made by a traditional manufacturer.
 A year later, Toyota added to its investment, partnering with Denso and the SoftBank Vision Fund to pour $1 billion into Uber’s self-driving effort. The deal valued Uber’s A.T.G. at  $7.25 billion. The cash infusion bought A.T.G. some time, but its long-term future at Uber remained unclear. The unit was expensive to operate, though Uber maintained it would become profitable by 2021.
 In 2019, when a  well-known start-up sold itself to Apple, the nascent autonomous vehicle industry began to consolidate.
 The coronavirus pandemic presented more problems. It curtailed testing because social-distancing rules prevented companies from keeping multiple drivers in cars to guard against accidents. Some industry veterans admitted that success was  further away than they had expected.
 The pandemic also put financial strain on Uber, which saw its core ride-hailing business decline as people stopped traveling. Investors had long pressured Mr. Khosrowshahi to sell the self-driving unit. The demands became more urgent as  Uber’s revenue plummeted.
  Aurora has its headquarters in Silicon Valley but also runs an office in Pittsburgh, where its chief technology officer, Drew Bagnell, a Carnegie Mellon professor and former Uber employee, works. As well as acquiring Uber’s self-driving technology it is acquiring engineers and support staff, most of whom are based in Pittsburgh.
 As part of the agreement between the two companies, Uber is taking a 26 percent stake in the start-up and Mr. Khosrowshahi will take a seat on the Aurora board of directors.
 “Uber can still have a hand in the game, without putting out the enormous amount of money they were spending with A.T.G., but still have a path to market when this technology is actually deploy-able in several years,” said Elliot Katz, co-founder and chief business officer of Phantom Auto, a start-up that helps companies remotely control autonomous vehicles.
 In 2018, Aurora agreed to supply self-driving technology to Volkswagen Group and Hyundai, two of the world’s largest car companies. But both Volkswagen and Hyundai have since embraced other partners amid the wider industry shakeout, and Volkswagen is no longer working with Aurora.
 For months, Mr. Urmson has said that his start-up’s first product would involve long-haul trucking. The Uber deal, he said, will not change that.
 “If you are a passenger in the back of a vehicle and the driver is too cautious, that can be frustrating, and if they are not cautious enough, that’s scary,” he said. “If you are a roll of toilet paper in the back of a truck, that is a different matter.”
title: 经过多年的尝试，优步正在实施其自动驾驶汽车项目
title_en: Uber, After Years of Trying, Is Handing Off Its Self-Driving Car Project

     伊朗当局对这位科学家在开车经过Absard镇时被枪gun的说法提出了相互矛盾的说法。
 袭击发生当天，国防部说，法赫扎扎德的保镖与几名持枪者之间发生了枪战。
  伊朗的一份报告援引目击者的话说，据说有4个人被恐怖分子杀害了3个人。据说还有日产的皮卡在现场爆炸。
 伊朗最高国家安全委员会主席在法赫扎扎德（Fakhrizadeh）葬礼上的讲话中说，这实际上是使用特殊方法进行的远程袭击。和＆＃34;电子设备＆＃34;。但他没有提供更多细节。
 革命卫队副司令法达维将军星期天在德黑兰举行的仪式上说，安装在日产卡车上的机枪上装有智能卫星系统，该系统放大了烈士法赫里扎德。和正在使用人工智能。 
他说，机关枪只聚焦在烈士法赫里扎德的脸上，以使他的妻子尽管只有25厘米（10英寸），却没有开枪。
 这位将军重申，现场没有人身袭击者，并说总共发射了13枚子弹，所有这些子弹都是从日产的武器中射出的。法赫里亚德（Fakhrizadeh）的安全负责人向自己投掷四枚子弹。他补充说。
 伊朗最高领导人阿亚图拉·阿里·哈梅内伊（Ayatollah Ali Khamenei）发誓要暗杀报仇，要求给予明确的惩罚。背后的人。
  周五，以色列公共广播电台报道说，以色列安全官员警告过一些前核科学家要谨慎。专家曾经在迪莫纳的反应堆工作，迪莫纳是内盖夫沙漠深处的一个秘密秘密核基地。
 以色列外交部要求以色列前往中东和非洲旅行的以色列公民要提高警惕，因为据称来自“伊朗分子”的威胁，以色列政府对此消息没有发表评论。
   关于使用这种尖端高科技武器进行攻击的说法令人震惊，因为它们是反乌托邦式的。
  在冲突中使用AI是一个使许多科学家感到担忧的概念。 2015年，已故的斯蒂芬·霍金教授是1,000位签署公开信的科学家之一，该信呼吁禁止发展用于军事用途的人工智能。 
专门从事电子战的分析师汤姆·威辛顿（Tom Withington）表示，应使用少量健康的食盐处理报告，并补充说，伊朗的描述似乎只不过是一些34;很酷的流行语＆＃34;旨在暗示只有一支强大的部队才能成功完成这项任务。
 反对杀手机器人运动成员诺埃尔·沙基（Noel Sharkey）教授说，使用这种武器的军事力量的后果将是“难以想象的后果”。
 ＆＃34;如果此类设备是自主的，使用面部识别来查明并杀死人员，我们将走上坡路，这将完全破坏全球安全，＆＃34;他说。
   Mohsen Fakhrizadeh是伊朗防御创新研究组织的负责人，该组织的波斯语缩写为SPND。
  他们相信物理学教授领导了“阿马德计划”（Project Amad），这是伊朗据称于1989年建立的一项秘密计划，旨在对潜在的核弹进行研究。
   然而，以色列总理内塔尼亚胡（Natanyahu）在2018年表示，该国获得的文件显示，法赫扎扎德（Fakhrizadeh）领导了一个正在秘密继续阿马德计划（Project Amad）工作的计划。
 伊朗坚持认为其核计划是完全和平的，并且从未寻求过核武器。 
分析人士猜测， Fakhrizadeh 的 暗杀 并不意味着 削弱 伊朗核计划 ，而是要 制止 美国 的前景 再结合 2015年 伊朗 核协议 时， 总统当选人 拜登 上任 明年 。
note_en:
A satellite-controlled machine-gun with "artificial intelligence" was used to kill Iran's top nuclear scientist, a Revolutionary Guards commander says.
  Brig-Gen Ali Fadavi told local media that the weapon, mounted in a pick-up truck, was able to fire at Fakhrizadeh without hitting his wife beside him. The claim could not be verified.
     The Iranian authorities have put out conflicting accounts of how the scientist was gunned down as he travelled in a car through the town of Absard.
 On the day of the attack, the defence ministry said there was  a gunfight between Fakhrizadeh's bodyguards and several gunmen.
  One Iranian report quoted witnesses as saying that "three to four individuals, who are said to have been terrorists, were killed". A Nissan pick-up was also said to have exploded at the scene.
 In a speech at Fakhrizadeh's funeral, the head of Iran's Supreme National Security Council said it was actually a remote attack, using "special methods" and "electronic equipment". But he provided no further details.
 Gen Fadavi, the deputy commander of the Revolutionary Guards, told a ceremony in Tehran on Sunday that a machine-gun mounted on the Nissan pick-up was "equipped with an intelligent satellite system which zoomed in on martyr Fakhrizadeh" and "was using artificial intelligence".
  The machine-gun "focused only on martyr Fakhrizadeh's face in a way that his wife, despite being only 25cm [10 inches] away, was not shot", he said.
 The general reiterated that no human assailants had been present at the scene, saying that "in total 13 bullets were fired and all of them were shot from the [weapon] in the Nissan". Four bullets struck Fakhrizadeh's head of security "as he threw himself" on the scientist, he added.
 Iran's Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, has vowed to avenge the assassination, demanding the "definitive punishment" of those behind it.
  On Friday, Israeli public radio reported that Israeli security officials had warned some former nuclear scientists to be cautious. The experts used to work at the reactor in Dimona, a top secret nuclear site deep in the Negev desert.
 The Israeli government did not comment on the report, which came a day after the Israeli foreign ministry told Israeli citizens travelling in the Middle East and Africa to be vigilant in light of what it called threats from "Iranian elements".
   The claims made about the attack being carried out using such a sophisticated high-tech weapon are as alarming as they are dystopian.
  The use of AI in conflict is a concept that has worried many scientists for some time. In 2015, the late Professor Stephen Hawking was one of 1,000 scientists who signed an open letter calling for a ban on the development of artificial intelligence for military use.
  Analyst Tom Withington, who specialises in electronic warfare, said the reports should be treated with "a healthy pinch of salt", and added that Iran's description appeared to be little more than a collection of "cool buzzwords" designed to suggest that only a supremely mighty force could possibly have succeeded in this mission.
 Professor Noel Sharkey, a member of the Campaign Against Killer Robots, said the consequences of military forces having access to such weapons would have "unimaginable consequences".
 "If such devices were autonomous, using face-recognition to pinpoint and kill people, we would be on a downhill roll that would entirely disrupt global security," he said.
   Mohsen Fakhrizadeh was head of Iran's Organisation of Defensive Innovation and Research, known by its Persian acronym SPND.
  They believe the physics professor led "Project Amad", a covert programme that Iran allegedly established in 1989 to carry out research on a potential nuclear bomb.
   However, Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu said in 2018 that documents obtained by his country showed Fakhrizadeh led a programme that was secretly continuing Project Amad's work.
 Iran insists its nuclear programme is entirely peaceful and that it has never sought a nuclear weapon.
 Analysts have speculated that assassination of Fakhrizadeh was not meant to cripple the Iranian nuclear programme but rather to put an end to the prospect of the US rejoining the 2015 Iran nuclear deal when President-elect Joe Biden takes office next year.

Aurora周一宣布，美国领先的自动驾驶初创公司之一Aurora将成为Uber自动驾驶部门的新所有人。除了接管Uber的自动驾驶部门Uber Advanced Technology Group（ATG）之外，Uber还将向Aurora注入4亿美元。
 作为交换，Uber将获得Aurora的少数股权，而Uber首席执行官Dara Khosrowshahi将获得Aurora董事会的席位。
 该协议使Uber可以卸载自驾车部门，自2018年3月Uber ATG车辆撞死一名行人以来，该部门一直在努力重新站稳脚跟.Uber在事件发生后的几个月内关闭了道路测试，自此以后，该计划就一直面临着公众的持续怀疑。目前尚不清楚这笔交易是否会导致优步ATG裁员。
  Aurora首席执行官Chris Urmson在卸任Google无人驾驶项目负责人后，于2016年共同创立了Aurora。他组建了一个“梦之队”。以及特斯拉（Tesla）和优步（Uber）无人驾驶项目的前领导人。这三人组建了一家因工程严谨而广受推崇的公司，尽管到目前为止，该公司尚未发布有关其自动驾驶技术性能的大量信息。
 Aurora的原始商业模式是将其自动驾驶技术许可给汽车制造商。但是近年来，越来越多的OEM收购了其他自动驾驶公司的股份，使Aurora在汽车行业的潜在合作伙伴很少。去年，大众汽车公司终止了旨在最终在大众汽车中采用Aurora技术的合作伙伴关系，从而使Aurora遭受了挫折。
 因此，在2019年晚些时候，Aurora转向长途卡车运输作为其自动驾驶技术的第一个应用程序。这种商业模式可以使Aurora在不与汽车制造商达成大笔交易的情况下获得成功。但这是有风险的，因为没人知道自动驾驶技术将在多长时间后为高速公路使用做好准备。
    优步的交易使Aurora在两个方面都得到了推动。该交易以4亿美元现金进行，这扩大了Aurora的跑道。达到盈利。优步在Aurora中的股份还增加了如果Aurora需要的话，Uber将来愿意提供更多现金的可能性。 
该交易还允许Aurora对冲长途卡车运输的押注。 Uber ATG的重点一直放在无人出租车上。 Aurora首席执行官Chris Urmson在博客中写道，该交易为Aurora提供了前所未有的机会来引领卡车和乘客出行领域的行业。 Uber ATG团队不仅会在出租车市场带来宝贵的专业知识，而且当Aurora最终准备在全国和全球范围内部署无人驾驶出租车时，Uber将成为宝贵的合作伙伴。
 几年前，Uber面临指控和诉讼，因为它试图从Waymo窃取激光雷达技术。 Aurora去年收购了激光雷达初创公司Blackmore，因此Aurora现在可以为未来的前Uber项目提供激光雷达技术。
  该交易的一个重要潜台词是认识到，自动驾驶技术的所有权可能并不是几年前看来对Uber的战略必要性。
 当Uber在2015年启动自动驾驶计划时，首席执行官Travis Kalanick将其视为公司生存之道。
 ＆＃34;如果我们不被并列，那么谁在第一，或者谁在第一，然后推出一个比Uber便宜得多或质量更高的乘车共享网络39; s，那么Uber不再是一回事，＆＃34;卡兰尼克在2016年的一次采访中告诉《商业内幕》。
 当时，对自动驾驶技术的大肆宣传已接近尾声。谷歌早在几年前就推出了自动驾驶技术，许多行业观察家预计自动驾驶技术将在2020年代初广泛使用。因此，Uber开始花费数百万美元来追赶Google。
    Uber的工作如此匆忙，以至于未能采取足够的安全预防措施，导致2018年致命的撞车事故致死行人Elaine Herzberg。回想起来，目前还不清楚Uber的自动驾驶项目在最初的两年中是否取得了如此大的进步。尽管Uber在2018年3月之前记录了很多测试里程（仅次于美国Waymo），但汽车驾驶行为有时不稳定。 
“ 2月的每隔一天”＆＃34;几乎2月的每隔一天，汽车就损坏了，＆＃34;一名Uber员工在2018年2月赫兹伯格去世前不久在一封严厉的内部电子邮件中写道。 ＆＃34;我们不应每15,000英里就撞东西。
 2018年发生车祸的部分原因是Uber禁用了一些车辆＆＃39;紧急制动系统。显然，这些系统过于频繁地接合，导致乘坐不舒服。相反，如果汽车出了错，该公司将依靠安全驾驶员进行干预。
 由此造成的崩溃迫使Uber停止了所有在路测试几个月，然后以较小的规模恢复测试。该程序再也没有恢复到坠机前的动力。
 在坠机事故发生时，我敦促Uber出售该部门-也许卖给一家大型汽车制造商。我认为，给该项目一个新的公司母公司，该公司的母公司以其内部的色彩闻名，可以帮助恢复公众的信任。而且我注意到，Uber远远没有盈利，并且没有无限的现金可投入到R＆amp; D项目中。
 最重要的是，我认为Uber的生存不依赖于拥有自动驾驶技术。我注意到，即使Waymo或其他人发明了全自动驾驶汽车，对于他们来说，从头开始建立全球乘车网络仍然是缓慢而昂贵的。因此，Uber将为想要大规模部署其自动驾驶技术的公司提供很多服务。
 两年半后，我认为这种分析方法很好。自动驾驶汽车进入市场的时间比乐观主义者预期的要长。该技术无处不在可能需要很多年。作为Aurora的少数股东，Uber可以成为潜在的无人驾驶技术合作伙伴，而不必强迫Uber承担开发自动驾驶技术的全部研发费用。

Aurora, one of the nation's leading self-driving startups, will become the new owner of Uber's self-driving division, Aurora  announced on Monday. In addition to turning over Uber's self-driving division, known as the Uber Advanced Technology Group (ATG), Uber will also pump $400 million into Aurora.
 In exchange, Uber will get a minority stake in Aurora and Uber CEO Dara Khosrowshahi will get a seat on Aurora's board.
 The deal allows Uber to unload a self-driving division that has struggled to regain its footing ever since an Uber ATG vehicle  struck and killed a pedestrian in March 2018. Uber shut down its on-road testing for several months after that incident, and the program has faced lingering public skepticism ever since. It's not clear if the deal will lead to layoffs at Uber ATG.
  Aurora CEO Chris Urmson co-founded Aurora in 2016 after leaving his role as the head of Google's self-driving project. He assembled a "dream team" along with former leaders at Tesla and Uber's self-driving projects. The trio built a company that's widely respected for its engineering rigor—though so far the company hasn't released much information about the performance of its self-driving technology.
 Aurora's original business model was to license its self-driving technology to automakers. But in recent years, more and more OEMs have taken stakes in other self-driving companies, leaving Aurora with few potential partners in the auto industry. Aurora  suffered a setback last year when Volkswagen ended a partnership intended to eventually put Aurora technology in Volkswagen vehicles.
 So later in 2019, Aurora  pivoted to long-haul trucking as the first application for its self-driving technology. This business model could enable Aurora to succeed without striking big deals with automakers. But it's risky, since no one knows how soon self-driving technology will be ready for high-speed highway use.
    The Uber deal gives Aurora a boost on a couple of fronts. The deal comes with $400 million in cash, which extends Aurora's "runway" to reach profitability. Uber's stake in Aurora also increases the likelihood that Uber will be willing to put up more cash in the future if Aurora needs it.
 The deal also allows Aurora to hedge its bet on long-haul trucking. Uber ATG's focus has been on driverless taxis. In a blog post, Aurora CEO Chris Urmson  wrote that the deal "gives Aurora an unprecedented opportunity to lead the industry in both trucking and passenger mobility." Not only does the Uber ATG team bring valuable expertise in the taxi market, but Uber will be a valuable partner when Aurora is eventually ready to deploy driverless taxis across the country and around the world.
 A few years ago, Uber faced accusations—and a  lawsuit—that it tried to steal lidar technology from Waymo. Aurora  acquired lidar startup Blackmore last year, so Aurora can now supply lidar technology to the former Uber project going forward.
  An important subtext of the deal is the realization that ownership of self-driving technology probably isn't the strategic necessity for Uber it seemed to be a few years ago.
 When Uber launched its self-driving program in 2015, CEO Travis Kalanick viewed it as a matter of survival for his company.
 "If we are not tied for first, then the person who is in first, or the entity that's in first, then rolls out a ride-sharing network that is far cheaper or far higher quality than Uber's, then Uber is no longer a thing,"  Kalanick told Business Insider in a 2016 interview.
 At the time, hype over self-driving technology was nearing its crescendo. Google had unveiled its self-driving technology a couple of years earlier, and many industry observers expected self-driving technology to be widely available by the early 2020s. And so Uber began spending millions of dollars in an effort to catch up to Google.
    Uber's effort was so rushed that it failed to take adequate safety precautions, leading to a fatal crash in 2018 that killed pedestrian Elaine Herzberg. And in retrospect, it's not clear that Uber's self-driving project made all that much progress in its first two years. While Uber logged a lot of testing miles before March 2018—second only to Waymo in the US—the cars' driving behavior was sometimes erratic.
 “Every other day in February” "A car was damaged nearly every other day in February," one  Uber staffer wrote in a scathing internal email in February 2018, shortly before Herzberg's death. "We shouldn’t be hitting things every 15,000 miles."
 The 2018 crash occurred in part because Uber had disabled some of the vehicles' emergency braking systems. Apparently the systems had been engaging too often, causing uncomfortable rides. Instead, the company relied on safety drivers to intervene if the car made a mistake.
 The resulting crash forced Uber to halt all on-road testing for a few months, then resumed testing on a much smaller scale. The program never regained the sense of momentum it had prior to the crash.
 At the time of the crash, I urged Uber to  sell the division—perhaps to a major automaker. I argued that giving the project a new corporate parent with a reputation for coloring inside the lines could help to restore public trust. And I noted that Uber was far from turning a profit and didn't have unlimited cash to throw into R&D projects.
 Most importantly, I argued that Uber's survival didn't depend on owning self-driving technology. I noted that even after Waymo or others invented a fully self-driving car, it would be slow and expensive for them to build a global ride-hailing network from scratch. So Uber would have a lot to offer companies looking to deploy their self-driving technology on a large scale.
 Two and a half years later, I think this analysis holds up well. Self-driving cars have taken longer to come to market than optimists predicted. It will likely take many more years for the technology to become ubiquitous. Being a minority stakeholder in Aurora gives Uber a potential driverless technology partner—without forcing Uber to shoulder the full R&D costs of developing self-driving technology.
title: 优步将自动驾驶项目出售给初创公司Aurora
title_en: Uber sells self-driving project to startup Aurora

 我想我的评论中最简单的总结是，我希望在完成《赛博朋克》后就可以玩了。 《巫师3》不仅广受赞誉，而且对其许多系统都提出了批评，而且随着时间的推移，它已经发展成为流派领先的游戏。赛博朋克具有这种潜力，但不可否认的是，在我演奏之前，我想让他们解决一些实际问题。如果您有耐心，我会至少给您几个月的时间，这样您就不会因为游戏中的最好而被最糟糕的事情宠坏了。我认为在将来的某个时候，赛博朋克将是游戏中的关键头衔，但现在还不是。我们只是希望它能在2077年之前实现。
